data.description: Persistent memory system for AI coding agents. Agent-agnostic Go binary with SQLite + FTS5, MCP server, HTTP API, CLI, and TUI. - Gentleman-Programming/engram

Local SQLite stays authoritative; cloud is replication/shared access only.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRecommended first path (local smoke):\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"docker compose -f docker-compose.cloud.yml up -d\nengram cloud config --server http://127.0.0.1:18080\nengram cloud enroll smoke-project\nengram sync --cloud --project smoke-project\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003edocker compose -f docker-compose.cloud.yml up -d\nengram cloud config --server http://127.0.0.1:18080\nengram cloud enroll smoke-project\nengram sync --cloud --project smoke-project\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCloud mode is always project-scoped (\u003ccode\u003e--project\u003c/code\u003e is required; \u003ccode\u003eengram sync --cloud --all\u003c/code\u003e is intentionally blocked).\n\u003ccode\u003eENGRAM_CLOUD_ALLOWED_PROJECTS\u003c/code\u003e is required for \u003ccode\u003eengram cloud serve\u003c/code\u003e in both token-auth and insecure modes. Set it to \u003ccode\u003e*\u003c/code\u003e to allow all projects (useful for dev/internal deploys) — this bypasses per-project name enforcement while still requiring a non-empty project on each request.\nKnown repairable cloud sync/upsert/canonicalization failures keep the original error visible and recommend the explicit \u003ccode\u003edoctor\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003erepair\u003c/code\u003e flow below; Engram never auto-applies repair from sync or autosync.\nFor blocked cloud sync, \u003ccode\u003etransport_failed\u003c/code\u003e, or legacy session directory repair, see \u003ca href=\"/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/engram-cloud/troubleshooting.md\"\u003eEngram Cloud Troubleshooting\u003c/a\u003e.\nIf cloud sync stays blocked after \u003ccode\u003edoctor\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003erepair\u003c/code\u003e, download the rescue helper and run the recommended exported-row repair:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"tools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --fix-exported \u0026lt;project\u0026gt;\nengram sync --cloud --project \u0026lt;project\u0026gt;\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003etools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --fix-exported \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026lt;\u003c/span\u003eproject\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026gt;\u003c/span\u003e\nengram sync --cloud --project \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026lt;\u003c/span\u003eproject\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026gt;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e--fix-exported\u003c/code\u003e repairs local exported \u003ccode\u003esessions[].directory\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eobservations[]\u003c/code\u003e required fields that can still break the final push after \u003ccode\u003edoctor\u003c/code\u003e reports ready. For sequential legacy \u003ccode\u003esync_mutations\u003c/code\u003e blockers, use \u003ccode\u003etools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --all \u0026lt;project\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAfter upgrading \u003ccode\u003eengram\u003c/code\u003e while an MCP client is already running:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"engram setup claude-code\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003eengram setup claude-code\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThen restart Claude Code so it reloads the Engram MCP subprocess and refreshed hook/config files. Updating the \u003ccode\u003eengram\u003c/code\u003e binary on disk does not replace an already-running stdio MCP process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUpgrade flow for existing local databases\u003c/strong\u003e (diagnose → repair → bootstrap → status):\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: use relative path string for plugin source in marketplace.json

The marketplace.json used an object format with `"type": "git-subdir"`
for the plugin source field. This does not conform to the Claude Code
marketplace schema, which expects either:
- A relative path string (for plugins within the same marketplace repo)
- An object with `"source"` as the discriminator key (for external repos)

Since the engram plugin lives inside this same repository at
`plugin/claude-code/`, the correct format is a relative path string:
`"./plugin/claude-code"`.

This caused `claude plugin marketplace add` and `engram setup claude-code`
to fail with: "Invalid schema: plugins.0.source: Invalid input"

title="fix(cloud): clarify autosync token error log and add UTF-8 PS1 output encoding (#421) (#454)

- Correct misleading log message in tryStartAutosync: token can come from
  cloud.json (file fallback via resolveCloudRuntimeConfig) or ENGRAM_CLOUD_TOKEN;
  the old message implied only the env var was checked, causing Windows users
  running under Task Scheduler to think file-based config was unsupported.
- Add TestTryStartAutosyncUsesFileToken to lock the file-token fallback path
  for autosync startup — the existing behavior was correct but untested.
- Set [Console]::OutputEncoding and InputEncoding to UTF-8 in the Windows
  PowerShell hook so non-ASCII characters in hook JSON payloads are not
  mangled by the default system codepage (CP1252/CP850)." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/commit/178a015f26effb8600135e1f019dcbb6c98e00c6">fix(cloud): clarify autosync guide (#409)

* docs: add team usage guide

Adds docs/TEAM-USAGE.md covering the scope mental model (project vs
personal), the lingua franca convention for shared memory, and the
sync behavior teams actually get today.

Honest about the current contract: scope filters search and context
reads but does not filter sync. Both scopes leave the machine when
the project is enrolled. Documents two workarounds (separate project
name or no enrollment) and flags scope-aware sync as a deliberate
future design decision.

Linked from DOCS.md, the README nav, and the mem_save scope parameter
description so the guide is discoverable from the obvious entry
points.

Closes #305

* docs(team-usage): clarify sync operates by project/session association per #331 review

---------

repairable cloud-upgrade mutations even when a blocker is queued (#446) (#452)

Previously RepairCloudUpgrade returned Applied:false and skipped all repairs
when BlockedCount > 0, even if RepairableCount was also > 0. The message also
always picked Findings[0] (lowest seq, often a repairable entry) instead of
the first non-repairable finding, and omitted entity_key.

Now the repairable subset is applied first inside the existing transaction
path; the blocked report then carries Applied:true, accurate counts, and a
message that scans Findings for the first non-repairable entry (with
entity_key) so operators see the actual blocker." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/commit/1858513b114c4a985f438eb214ec238406bccb3c">fix(store): apply repairable class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(cloud): clarify autosync token error log and add UTF-8 PS1 output encoding (#421) (#454)

- Correct misleading log message in tryStartAutosync: token can come from
  cloud.json (file fallback via resolveCloudRuntimeConfig) or ENGRAM_CLOUD_TOKEN;
  the old message implied only the env var was checked, causing Windows users
  running under Task Scheduler to think file-based config was unsupported.
- Add TestTryStartAutosyncUsesFileToken to lock the file-token fallback path
  for autosync startup — the existing behavior was correct but untested.
- Set [Console]::OutputEncoding and InputEncoding to UTF-8 in the Windows
  PowerShell hook so non-ASCII characters in hook JSON payloads are not
  mangled by the default system codepage (CP1252/CP850)." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/commit/178a015f26effb8600135e1f019dcbb6c98e00c6">fix(cloud): clarify autosync class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="docs: add team usage guide (#409)

* docs: add team usage guide

Adds docs/TEAM-USAGE.md covering the scope mental model (project vs
personal), the lingua franca convention for shared memory, and the
sync behavior teams actually get today.

Honest about the current contract: scope filters search and context
reads but does not filter sync. Both scopes leave the machine when
the project is enrolled. Documents two workarounds (separate project
name or no enrollment) and flags scope-aware sync as a deliberate
future design decision.

Linked from DOCS.md, the README nav, and the mem_save scope parameter
description so the guide is discoverable from the obvious entry
points.

Closes #305

class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="docs(beta): add isolated docker-compose + community testing guide

For Phases 2 + 3 + 4 community beta. The setup runs an isolated cloud
(postgres on 25432, engram cloud on 28080) with separate container
names, separate volume, and a token gate — leaving any existing
engram-cloud setup at the default ports/data dir/binary completely
untouched.

  - docker-compose.beta.yml — engram-beta-postgres + engram-beta-cloud
    on non-default ports, beta-only allowed-projects allowlist
  - docs/BETA_TESTING.md — step-by-step testing guide covering:
    * isolated env setup (ENGRAM_DATA_DIR + ENGRAM_CLOUD_SERVER)
    * Phase 2 verification: cross-machine relation sync via 2 data dirs
    * Phase 3 verification: engram conflicts CLI + HTTP API
    * Phase 4 verification: --semantic with user's existing
      claude/opencode CLI ($0 on subscription plans)
    * mem_compare MCP tool
    * full cleanup (down -v + rm tmp dirs) leaves prod untouched

Testers clone the branch, run compose, build a local ./engram-beta
binary, and exercise the full stack without affecting their existing
engram cloud or ~/.engram/engram.db." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/commit/585ab7dfd3a23540fe9135c0af8f2d17b236ccfc">docs(beta): add isolated docker-compose + community entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><p align="center" dir="auto">
  <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/assets/branding/engram-banner.png"><img width="1024" alt="Engram — One Brain. Local or Cloud." src="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/raw/main/assets/branding/engram-banner.png" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <strong>Persistent memory for AI coding agents</strong><br>
  <em>One brain. Local or cloud. Agent-agnostic, single binary, zero dependencies.</em>
</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/INSTALLATION.md">Installation</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/engram-cloud/README.md">Engram Cloud</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/AGENT-SETUP.md">Agent Setup</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/CODEBASE-GUIDE.md">Codebase Guide</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md">Architecture</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/PLUGINS.md">Plugins</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/TEAM-USAGE.md">Team Usage</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md">Contributing</a> •
  <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/DOCS.md">Full Docs</a>
</p>
<hr>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>engram</strong> <code>/ˈen.ɡræm/</code> — <em>neuroscience</em>: the physical trace of a memory in the brain.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Your AI coding agent forgets everything when the session ends. Engram gives it a brain.</p>
<p dir="auto">A <strong>Go binary</strong> with SQLite + FTS5 full-text search, exposed via CLI, HTTP API, MCP server, and an interactive TUI. Works with <strong>any agent</strong> that supports MCP — Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex, VS Code (Copilot), Antigravity, Cursor, Windsurf, or anything else.</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Agent (Claude Code / OpenCode / Gemini CLI / Codex / VS Code / Antigravity / ...)
    ↓ MCP stdio
Engram (single Go binary)
    ↓
SQLite + FTS5 (~/.engram/engram.db)
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<th>Agent</th>
<th>One-liner</th>

<tr>
<td>Claude Code</td>
<td><code>claude plugin marketplace add Gentleman-Programming/engram && claude plugin install engram</code></td>

<tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<td>Codex</td>
<td><code>engram setup codex</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>VS Code</td>
<td><code>code --add-mcp '{"name":"engram","command":"engram","args":["mcp"]}'</code></td>
</tr>

<p dir="auto">Full per-agent config, Memory Protocol, and compaction survival → <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/AGENT-SETUP.md">docs/AGENT-SETUP.md</a></p>
<p dir="auto">That's it. No Node.js, no Python, no Docker. <strong>One binary, one SQLite file.</strong></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Pi Package</h3><a id="user-content-pi-package" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Pi Package" href="#pi-package"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">It gives Pi persistent project memory, compaction recovery, and shared memory with other MCP agents through the same local-or-cloud Engram brain. The package is part of the Gentleman Programming agentic-coding ecosystem alongside Gentle-AI, SDD, skills, and Engram Cloud.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How It Works</h2><a id="user-content-how-it-works" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How It Works" href="#how-it-works"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>1. Agent completes significant work (bugfix, architecture decision, etc.)
2. Agent calls mem_save → title, type, What/Why/Where/Learned
3. Engram persists to SQLite with FTS5 indexing
4. Next session: agent searches memory, gets relevant context
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Category</th>
<th>Tools</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Save & Update</strong></td>
<td><code>mem_save</code>, <code>mem_update</code>, <code>mem_delete</code>, <code>mem_suggest_topic_key</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Search & Retrieve</strong></td>
<td><code>mem_search</code>, <code>mem_context</code>, <code>mem_timeline</code>, <code>mem_get_observation</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Session Lifecycle</strong></td>
<td><code>mem_session_start</code>, <code>mem_session_end</code>, <code>mem_session_summary</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Conflict Surfacing</strong></td>
<td><code>mem_judge</code>, <code>mem_compare</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Utilities</strong></td>
<td><code>mem_save_prompt</code>, <code>mem_stats</code>, <code>mem_capture_passive</code>, <code>mem_merge_projects</code>, <code>mem_current_project</code>, <code>mem_doctor</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Git Sync</h2><a id="user-content-git-sync" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Git Sync" href="#git-sync"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Share memories across machines. Uses compressed chunks — no merge conflicts, no huge files.</p>
<p dir="auto">Local SQLite remains the source of truth. Cloud integration is opt-in replication.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>engram sync                    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Export new memories as compressed chunk</span>
git add .engram/ <span class="pl-k">&&</span> git commit -m <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sync engram memories<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
engram sync --import           <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> On another machine: import new chunks</span>
engram sync --status           <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Check sync status</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="engram sync                    # Export new memories as compressed chunk
git add .engram/ && git commit -m "sync engram memories"
engram sync --import           # On another machine: import new chunks
engram sync --status           # Check sync status" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cloud Integration (Opt-In Replication)</h2><a id="user-content-cloud-integration-opt-in-replication" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cloud Integration (Opt-In Replication)" href="#cloud-integration-opt-in-replication"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Cloud is optional. Local SQLite stays authoritative; cloud is replication/shared access only.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Recommended first path (local smoke):</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>docker compose -f docker-compose.cloud.yml up -d
engram cloud config --server http://127.0.0.1:18080
engram cloud enroll smoke-project
engram sync --cloud --project smoke-project</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Cloud mode is always project-scoped (<code>--project</code> is required; <code>engram sync --cloud --all</code> is intentionally blocked).
<code>ENGRAM_CLOUD_ALLOWED_PROJECTS</code> is required for <code>engram cloud serve</code> in both token-auth and insecure modes. Set it to <code>*</code> to allow all projects (useful for dev/internal deploys) — this bypasses per-project name enforcement while still requiring a non-empty project on each request.
Known repairable cloud sync/upsert/canonicalization failures keep the original error visible and recommend the explicit <code>doctor</code>/<code>repair</code> flow below; Engram never auto-applies repair from sync or autosync.
For blocked cloud sync, <code>transport_failed</code>, or legacy session directory repair, see <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/engram/blob/main/docs/engram-cloud/troubleshooting.md">Engram Cloud Troubleshooting</a>.
If cloud sync stays blocked after <code>doctor</code>/<code>repair</code>, download the rescue helper and run the recommended exported-row repair:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>tools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --fix-exported <span class="pl-k"><</span>project<span class="pl-k">></span>
engram sync --cloud --project <span class="pl-k"><</span>project<span class="pl-k">></span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="tools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --fix-exported <project>
engram sync --cloud --project <project>" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><code>--fix-exported</code> repairs local exported <code>sessions[].directory</code> and <code>observations[]</code> required fields that can still break the final push after <code>doctor</code> reports ready. For sequential legacy <code>sync_mutations</code> blockers, use <code>tools/repair-missing-session-directory.sh --apply --interactive --all <project></code>.</p>

<p dir="auto">Then restart Claude Code so it reloads the Engram MCP subprocess and refreshed hook/config files. Updating the <code>engram</code> binary on disk does not replace an already-running stdio MCP process.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Upgrade flow for existing local databases</strong> (diagnose → repair → bootstrap → status):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>engram cloud upgrade doctor --project smoke-project        <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> read-only readiness check</span>
engram cloud upgrade repair --project smoke-project --dry-run
engram cloud upgrade repair --project smoke-project --apply
engram cloud upgrade bootstrap --project smoke-project     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> resumable enroll + push + verify</span>
engram cloud upgrade status --project smoke-project        <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> stage/class/reason</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Steps to Test (Beta — Phases 2+3+4)</h2><a id="user-content-steps-to-test-beta--phases-234" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Steps to Test (Beta — Phases 2+3+4)" href="#steps-to-test-beta--phases-234"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Try the new memory-conflict-surfacing features in <strong>complete isolation</strong> from your existing engram setup. Docker uses non-default ports + a separate data dir + a beta-only token, so your prod cloud and <code>~/.engram/</code> are untouched. Cleanup is one command.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What's in the beta</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>🔄 Cloud sync of conflict relations cross-machine</li>
<li>🔍 <code>engram conflicts</code> CLI + HTTP API for retroactive audit + scan</li>
<li>🧠 <code>--semantic</code> scan that uses <strong>your existing Claude Code or OpenCode CLI</strong> to judge FTS5 conflict candidates with LLM reasoning — <strong>$0 if you're on a Pro/Max/Plus subscription</strong></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Setup (4 commands)</h3><a id="user-content-setup-4-commands" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Setup (4 commands)" href="#setup-4-commands"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>git clone https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram.git engram-beta-repo
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> engram-beta-repo <span class="pl-k">&&</span> git checkout feat/memory-conflict-surfacing-cloud-sync
docker compose -f docker-compose.beta.yml up -d
go build -o ./engram-beta ./cmd/engram

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Isolated env (does NOT touch ~/.engram or your prod cloud)</span>
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ENGRAM_DATA_DIR=/tmp/engram-beta-data
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ENGRAM_CLOUD_SERVER=http://127.0.0.1:28080
<span class="pl-k">export</span> ENGRAM_CLOUD_TOKEN=beta-token-CHANGE-ME-please-32chars

  <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Use Hexagonal Architecture<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> \

  --type architecture --project beta-test</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto"><strong>5️⃣ The case where FTS5 finds a candidate, then the LLM judges meaning</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">Lexically related candidate titles with a semantic conflict:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>./engram-beta save \
  <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Use Postgres for the user database<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> \
  <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Postgres 15 is our SQL store for users.<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> \
  --type architecture --project beta-test

./engram-beta save \
  <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Replace the user database with MongoDB<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> \
  <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Document store now backs the user collection. SQL is gone.<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> \
  --type decision --project beta-test

./engram-beta conflicts scan --project beta-test --semantic --apply \
  --max-semantic 5 --yes

./engram-beta conflicts list --project beta-test --status judged</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Command</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>engram setup [agent]</code></td>
<td>Install agent integration</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram serve [port]</code></td>
<td>Start HTTP API (default: 7437)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram mcp [--tools=PROFILE] [--project NAME]</code></td>
<td>Start MCP server (stdio transport)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram tui</code></td>
<td>Launch terminal UI</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram search <query></code></td>
<td>Search memories</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram save <title> <msg></code></td>
<td>Save a memory</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram delete <obs_id></code></td>
<td>Delete an observation (soft by default; <code>--hard</code> removes permanently)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram delete session <id></code></td>
<td>Delete a session by ID (must have no observations)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram delete prompt <id></code></td>
<td>Delete a prompt by ID (permanent)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram delete project <name> [--hard]</code></td>
<td>Cascade-delete a project: soft-deletes observations by default (<code>--hard</code> removes permanently and also removes sessions)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram timeline <obs_id></code></td>
<td>Chronological context</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram context [project]</code></td>
<td>Recent session context</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram stats</code></td>
<td>Memory statistics</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram export [file]</code></td>
<td>Export to JSON</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram import <file></code></td>
<td>Import from JSON</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram sync</code></td>
<td>Git sync export/import</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram conflicts <sub></code></td>
<td>Inspect and manage memory conflict relations</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram doctor</code></td>
<td>Run read-only operational diagnostics</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram cloud <subcommand></code></td>
<td>Opt-in cloud config/status/enrollment + cloud runtime (<code>serve</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram projects list|consolidate|prune</code></td>
<td>Manage project names</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram obsidian-export</code></td>
<td>Export to Obsidian vault (beta)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>engram version</code></td>
<td>Show version</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Environment Variables</h3><a id="user-content-key-environment-variables" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Environment Variables" href="#key-environment-variables"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Variable</th>
<th>Description</th>
<th>Default</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_DATA_DIR</code></td>
<td>Override data directory</td>
<td><code>~/.engram</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_PORT</code></td>
<td>Override HTTP server port</td>
<td><code>7437</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_HTTP_TOKEN</code></td>
<td>Optional Bearer auth for local HTTP server. When set, destructive and export routes require <code>Authorization: Bearer <token></code>. Unset = open (zero-config default).</td>
<td>(unset)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_TIMEZONE</code></td>
<td>Timezone for timestamp display in TUI and cloud dashboard (e.g. <code>America/New_York</code>). Falls back to system local when unset or invalid.</td>
<td>system local</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_CLOUD_AUTOSYNC</code></td>
<td>Set to <code>1</code> to enable background autosync (also requires <code>ENGRAM_CLOUD_TOKEN</code> + <code>ENGRAM_CLOUD_SERVER</code>).</td>
<td>(unset)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ENGRAM_CLOUD_ALLOWED_PROJECTS</code></td>
<td>Comma-separated project allowlist for <code>engram cloud serve</code>. Use <code>*</code> to allow all projects.</td>
<td>(unset)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

      </p>
